name: Santo Tomé y Cerró de las Albahacas — circular
region_intro: Jogging around Santo Tomé, Corrientes, Argentina, offers diverse environments for active recreation. The region features revitalized riverfront areas along the Uruguay River, providing smooth, paved surfaces for urban running. Additionally, natural reserves like Taji Poty offer trails through rich biodiversity, suitable for trail jogging on potentially uneven terrain. The general green landscape and riverside location contribute to a pleasant environment for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Santo Tomé The most popular jogging route is Aguascebas Reservoir Loop, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail that takes about 1 hour to complete. This moderate route features varied terrain around the reservoir. Another top favourite among local runners is Santo Tomé y Cerró de las Albahacas — circular, a moderate 5.6 miles (9.1 km) path. This route offers a circular journey with notable elevation changes. Local runners also love the Round trip on the Paseo de Vida path — banks of the Río Guadalquivir, a 3.0 miles (4.8 km) trail leading through riverside landscapes, often completed in about 35 minutes. Jogging around Santo Tomé is defined by revitalized riverfront promenades, natural reserve trails, and green landscapes. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside paths to more challenging trails with elevation. The routes in Santo Tomé are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 9 reviews. More than 190 runners have used komoot to explore Santo Tomé's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Santo Tomé?
A: Komoot offers over a dozen running routes around Santo Tomé. These routes cater to various preferences, including easy riverside paths and more challenging trails with elevation changes.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes suitable for beginners in Santo Tomé?
A: Yes, Santo Tomé has options for beginners. An excellent easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1364657566/round-trip-on-the-paseo-de-vida-path-banks-of-the-rio-guadalquivir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Round trip on the Paseo de Vida path — banks of the Río Guadalquivir&lt;/a&gt;. This 4.8 km (3.0 miles) trail takes about 35 minutes to complete and offers pleasant riverside landscapes.

Q: What are the best times of year to go jogging in Santo Tomé?
A: The mild temperatures during spring and fall make these seasons ideal for outdoor exercise like jogging in Santo Tomé. The pleasant weather ensures a comfortable experience on the trails and promenades.

Q: Can I find circular running routes in the Santo Tomé area?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Santo Tomé are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1367495309/santo-tome-y-cerro-de-las-albahacas-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santo Tomé y Cerró de las Albahacas — circular&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 9.1 km (5.6 miles) path with notable elevation changes.

Q: Are there any jogging trails with waterfalls or scenic viewpoints?
A: Yes, the region offers scenic trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7471458" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de la Puerta Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2613975" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de los Órganos Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; are notable natural attractions. Additionally, the Reserva Natural Municipal Taji Poty features trails that lead to a viewpoint and a waterfall, offering beautiful natural vistas.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes in Santo Tomé?
A: Santo Tomé offers a mix of terrains. You'll find smooth, paved surfaces along the revitalized riverfront areas like the Paseo Costero La Islita y El Piñeral, perfect for urban running. For those who prefer natural, potentially uneven terrain, reserves like Taji Poty provide trails through rich biodiversity.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, for experienced runners seeking a challenge, there are more difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24525582" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Aguas Negras loop from Aparcamiento Rio Borosa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 21.8 km (13.5 miles) route that takes approximately 3 hours to complete, featuring significant elevation changes.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Santo Tomé?
A: The running routes in Santo Tomé are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 9 reviews. Runners often praise the varied terrain, from pleasant riverside paths to more challenging trails, and the beautiful natural surroundings.

Q: Are there any specific natural reserves or parks suitable for trail running?
A: The Reserva Natural Municipal Taji Poty is an excellent option for trail running. Located near the Uruguay River, it offers trails winding through rich biodiversity, including 200 species of birds and plants, and even carayá monkeys. It's about 1,000 meters from the urban center.

Q: Can I combine my run with sightseeing or visiting landmarks?
A: Many routes offer opportunities for sightseeing. Along some trails, you might encounter landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/183420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Elías Gorge&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3254605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de La Iruela&lt;/a&gt;. The coastal promenade also provides great views of the Uruguay River.

Q: Is there a good place for urban jogging with river views?
A: Absolutely. The Paseo Costero La Islita y El Piñeral offers a modern, well-lit coastal promenade along the Uruguay River. It features a paved road and sidewalks, making it ideal for urban jogging while enjoying majestic river views. This area is actively used by locals for physical activities.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by interesting man-made structures or historical sites?
A: Yes, some routes might lead you near interesting structures. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4282795" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Borosa River Water Canal Tunnel&lt;/a&gt; is a unique man-made highlight in the area that you could encounter on certain trails.
tips: Santo Tomé is located on the slopes of the Sierra de Cazorla, in the valley of the Guadalquivir river. passing the center of the village you can see the bell tower of the stone church, dating from the fourteenth century.
